rationale: The cited paper is used repeatedly in the citing article's theory development, not just in a background list. The citing paper draws on Berger and Milkman (2012) to motivate expectations that affect-laden or valenced content is more likely to be shared, and later uses it again to interpret its retransmission findings (positive and emotional/arousing content patterns). At the same time, the citing paper explicitly positions itself as moving beyond prior work like Berger and Milkman by examining conversation-level WOM characteristics (valence, channel, tie strength) and dual outcomes (purchase and retransmission intentions).
relation_description: The citing paper extends Berger and Milkman (2012) from online content virality to brand-focused WOM conversations, testing how valence effects interact with channel and social tie strength. It also broadens the outcome space by examining purchase intentions alongside retransmission intentions.
